an1.41:1.1: "Just as, *bhikkhus*, a rice spike or a barley spike wrongly directed — that it will pierce the hand or foot, or draw blood, when pressed upon by hand or foot: that is not possible.
an1.41:1.2: What is the reason for that?
an1.41:1.3: Because of the wrong direction of the spike, *bhikkhus*.
an1.41:1.4: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, that a *bhikkhu* with a wrongly directed mind [*citta*] will pierce blindness [*avijjā*], give rise to knowledge, and realize *nibbāna* — that is not possible.
an1.41:1.5: What is the reason for that?
an1.41:1.6: Because of the wrong direction of the mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*."
an1.42:1.1: "Just as, *bhikkhus*, a rice spike or a barley spike rightly directed — that it will pierce the hand or foot, or draw blood, when pressed upon by hand or foot: that is possible.
an1.42:1.2: What is the reason for that?
an1.42:1.3: Because of the right direction of the spike, *bhikkhus*.
an1.42:1.4: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, that a *bhikkhu* with a rightly directed mind [*citta*] will pierce blindness [*avijjā*], give rise to knowledge, and realize *nibbāna* — that is possible.
an1.42:1.5: What is the reason for that?
an1.42:1.6: Because of the right direction of the mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*."
an1.43:1.1: "Here, *bhikkhus*, I know a certain person with a corrupted mind [*citta*], having encompassed their mind [*citta*] with my own mind [*citta*], thus:
an1.43:1.2: 'If this person were to die at this very moment, they would be deposited accordingly in hell, as if carried there.'
an1.43:1.3: What is the reason for that?
an1.43:1.5: It is on account of corruption of mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, that some beings here, at the breakup of the body after death, are reborn in a state of loss, an unhappy destination, a downfall, in hell."
an1.44:1.1: "Here, *bhikkhus*, I know a certain person with a serene mind [*citta*], having encompassed their mind [*citta*] with my own mind [*citta*], thus:
an1.44:1.2: 'If this person were to die at this very moment, they would be deposited accordingly in heaven, as if carried there.'
an1.44:1.3: What is the reason for that?
an1.44:1.5: It is on account of serenity of mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, that some beings here, at the breakup of the body after death, are reborn in a happy destination, in a heavenly world."
an1.45:1.1: "Just as, *bhikkhus*, a lake that is turbid, stirred up, and muddy — a person with good eyesight standing on the bank would not see the oysters and conchs, the pebbles and gravel, or the schools of fish moving about and standing still.
an1.45:1.2: What is the reason for that?
an1.45:1.3: Because of the turbidity of the water, *bhikkhus*.
an1.45:1.4: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, that a *bhikkhu* with a turbid mind [*citta*] will know his own welfare, or another's welfare, or the welfare of both, or will realize a distinction in knowledge and vision surpassing the human, worthy of the noble ones — that is not possible.
an1.45:1.5: What is the reason for that?
an1.45:1.6: Because of the turbidity of the mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*."
an1.46:1.1: "Just as, *bhikkhus*, a lake that is clear, limpid, and undisturbed — a person with good eyesight standing on the bank would see the oysters and conchs, the pebbles and gravel, and the schools of fish moving about and standing still.
an1.46:1.2: What is the reason for that?
an1.46:1.3: Because of the clarity of the water, *bhikkhus*.
an1.46:1.4: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, that a *bhikkhu* with an undisturbed mind [*citta*] will know his own welfare, or another's welfare, or the welfare of both, or will realize a distinction in knowledge and vision surpassing the human, worthy of the noble ones — that is possible.
an1.46:1.5: What is the reason for that?
an1.46:1.6: Because of the clarity of the mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*."
an1.47:1.1: "Just as, *bhikkhus*, among all the various plant species, the phandana creeper is declared to be foremost for its pliancy and workability.
an1.47:1.2: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, I do not see a single other thing that, when developed and cultivated, becomes as pliant and workable as the mind [*citta*].
an1.47:1.3: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, becomes pliant and workable."
an1.48:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, a single other thing that changes as quickly as the mind [*citta*].
an1.48:1.2: It is not easy, *bhikkhus*, to find even a simile for how quickly the mind [*citta*] changes."
an1.49:1.2: Yet it is defiled by adventitious defilements."
an1.50:1.2: And it is freed from adventitious defilements."
